observed_mechanics.name: Tile Swapping
observed_mechanics.description: The player drags an adjacent object (like a leaf or berry) into a neighboring cell to swap them, attempting to form a straight line of 3 or more identical items.

observed_mechanics.name: Chain Reaction Cascade
observed_mechanics.description: After a successful match, the matched items disappear, and items above them fall down by gravity to fill the empty slots, sometimes triggering automatic subsequent matches.

observed_mechanics.name: Power-up Activation
observed_mechanics.description: The player taps a glowing tile (created by matching 4 items earlier) which instantly clears an entire horizontal and vertical row.

ui_elements_observed: Score progress bar at top center
Level target indicator showing collected items

playable_candidates.concept_one_liner: A single, highly-curated puzzle scenario where the player is one move away from a massive satisfying cascade.
playable_candidates.hook_first_3_seconds: A glowing hand pointer repeatedly drags a red berry tile over a green leaf tile, showing the player exactly what to do.

core_interaction.what_player_does: Drag the red berry tile to swap it with the green leaf tile to complete a 4-match.
core_interaction.win_state: Completing 3 moves successfully, which drops the target item to the bottom of the board.

end_card.headline: Great Job!
end_card.visual_treatment: Screen darkens, confetti falls from the top, and the green CTA button pulses in the center.

predicted_appeal.rationale: Forcing a massive cascade on the first move guarantees immediate visual satisfaction and hooks the user. It is very standard but highly effective for puzzle ads.

playable_candidates.implementation_notes_for_codegen: Hardcode the grid layout and the falling tile sequence rather than building a full RNG match-3 engine. Pre-calculate the cascade state to keep code size small.

playable_candidates.concept_one_liner: A frantic, time-attack version of the game utilizing only power-up tapping.
playable_candidates.hook_first_3_seconds: The board is entirely filled with regular tiles and 5 glowing bomb power-ups, with a prominent 'Tap Fast!' text pulsing over the board.

core_interaction.what_player_does: Tap the glowing bomb power-ups to clear sections of the board before a 10-second timer runs out.
core_interaction.win_state: Tapping all 5 power-ups within the time limit.
core_interaction.lose_state: Failing to tap all power-ups before the timer hits zero.

end_card.headline: Can You Beat It?
end_card.visual_treatment: A shattered glass effect overlays the screen with the CTA button appearing from below.

predicted_appeal.rationale: It distills the most explosive part of the game (power-ups) into a pure dopamine hit. The sense of urgency drives immediate interaction.

playable_candidates.implementation_notes_for_codegen: No need for a matching algorithm; just implement a grid of static images and 5 interactive buttons that trigger row/column clear animations.

playable_candidates.concept_one_liner: A 'fail state' bait ad where the player must clear a path for a character by swapping the correct tiles, but an obvious trap exists.
playable_candidates.hook_first_3_seconds: A cute animal character at the top of the screen is crying, blocked by rocks. A prompt asks 'Help him down!'.

core_interaction.what_player_does: Drag tiles to match them underneath the character so it falls to the exit at the bottom.
core_interaction.win_state: Matching the correct tiles to free the character.
core_interaction.lose_state: Matching the wrong tiles, causing rocks to fall and trap the character permanently.

end_card.headline: He Needs You!
end_card.visual_treatment: Character looking sad through a window overlay, with the bright CTA button below.

predicted_appeal.rationale: Narrative-driven puzzle ads with high stakes (saving a character) tend to convert very well, especially if the player thinks they are smarter than the simulated ad player.

playable_candidates.implementation_notes_for_codegen: Requires custom state logic for the character entity falling through the grid. Keep the grid small (e.g., 5x5) to simplify the physics and state management.

recommendation_rationale: Candidate A provides the most authentic representation of the core match-3 gameplay while guaranteeing a highly satisfying player experience. By hardcoding the cascade, it remains technically feasible within the 5MB single-file HTML limit while still feeling like a full game.

critical.name: Green Leaf Tile
critical.description_for_image_gen: A glossy, stylized bright green leaf, 3D render style, top-down perspective, soft shadow underneath, transparent background, 256x256.

critical.name: Red Berry Tile
critical.description_for_image_gen: A cluster of three shiny red berries, 3D render style, top-down perspective, soft shadow underneath, transparent background, 256x256.

critical.name: Forest Background
critical.description_for_image_gen: A blurred, lush forest clearing at daytime, soft lighting, vibrant greens and browns, cartoon style, 1080x1920.

critical.name: Tutorial Hand Pointer
critical.description_for_image_gen: A stylized, white cartoon glove pointing with its index finger, thick outline, slight drop shadow, transparent background, 256x256.

nice_to_have.name: Match Sparkles
nice_to_have.description_for_image_gen: A small glowing yellow star sparkle with a soft halo.
nice_to_have.fallback_if_missing: CSS box-shadow glow effect or yellow circle primitives.

technical_constraints.framework_rationale: A simple grid of tiles can be heavily optimized using standard HTML/CSS grid and CSS transitions, keeping the payload well under 5MB without the overhead of a canvas engine.
known_risks: Implementing a full match-3 logic engine is too heavy; must rely on a hardcoded sequence of moves.
CSS animations for falling tiles might look jittery on low-end Android devices if not utilizing hardware acceleration (transform: translate).

must_pass: Dragging a tile swaps it visually with the adjacent tile within 150ms
If a match of 3 or more is formed, the tiles disappear and trigger the cascade animation
Total HTML file size including base64 assets is strictly under 5MB
The end card and 'Play Now' button appear immediately after the first cascade finishes
should_pass: The visual style is recognizably similar to the vibrant, nature-themed aesthetic of the source video
The tutorial hand animates in a continuous loop until the user makes their first interaction

things_unobservable_from_videos: Whether the game features an overworld meta-layer or base-building mechanics
The monetization strategy or premium currency types
Audio if the videos were recorded without sound
